title: agastache palmeri var. breviflora
intro: Agastache palmeri var. breviflora, also known as Palmer's agastache, is a perennial herb native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Palmer's agastache include its ability to treat respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, as well as its potential to reduce fever and soothe digestive issues. Palmer's agastache can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and its essential oil can be used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and ease respiratory congestion.
